001package applicationMain;
002
003import entityClasses.Post;
004import entityClasses.PostList;
005import entityClasses.Reply;
006import entityClasses.ReplyList;
007
008/**
009 * Semi-automated validation test suite for TP2 student post and reply workflows.
010 *
011 * <p><strong>Purpose (Task 6):</strong> provide executable evidence that CRUD and validation
012 * behaviors satisfy the intent of Students User Stories.
013 *
014 * <p><strong>Requirement list covered in this class:</strong>
015 * <ol>
016 *   <li>R1: Create post succeeds for valid input.</li>
017 *   <li>R2: Create post rejects duplicate IDs.</li>
018 *   <li>R3: Create post rejects invalid title input.</li>
019 *   <li>R4: Read post by id returns existing post.</li>
020 *   <li>R5: Update post succeeds for valid target and data.</li>
021 *   <li>R6: Update post rejects missing target.</li>
022 *   <li>R7: Delete post succeeds for existing target.</li>
023 *   <li>R8: Delete post reports false for missing target.</li>
024 *   <li>R9: Create reply succeeds when post reference exists.</li>
025 *   <li>R10: Create reply rejects non-existent post reference.</li>
026 *   <li>R11: Update reply rejects invalid body.</li>
027 *   <li>R12: Subset search returns expected post/reply subsets.</li>
028 * </ol>
029 *
030 * <p><strong>How to interpret output:</strong>
031 * each test prints PASS or FAIL. The summary prints total passed/failed counts.
032 * A requirement is considered satisfied when its mapped test prints PASS and summary shows
033 * zero unexpected failures.
034 */
035public class HW2CrudValidationTest {
036
037        private static int passed = 0;
038        private static int failed = 0;
039
040        /**
041         * Executes all built-in test cases in deterministic order.
042         *
043         * @param args command-line arguments (not used)
044         */
045        public static void main(String[] args) {
046                System.out.println("=== HW2 CRUD + Validation Tests ===\n");
047
048                PostList postList = new PostList();
049                ReplyList replyList = new ReplyList();
050
051                testCreatePostSuccess(postList);
052                testCreatePostDuplicateId(postList);
053                testCreatePostInvalidTitle(postList);
054                testReadPostById(postList);
055                testUpdatePostSuccess(postList);
056                testUpdatePostMissing(postList);
057                testDeletePostSuccess(postList);
058                testDeletePostMissing(postList);
059
060                testCreateReplySuccess(postList, replyList);
061                testCreateReplyInvalidPostReference(postList, replyList);
062                testUpdateReplyInvalidBody(replyList);
063                testSubsetSearch(postList, replyList);
064
065                System.out.println("\n=== Test Summary ===");
066                System.out.println("Passed: " + passed);
067                System.out.println("Failed: " + failed);
068        }
069
070        /**
071         * Test ID: T1 (maps to R1).
072         *
073         * <p>Creates a valid post and verifies no error is returned.
074         * PASS means create behavior accepts valid student input.
075         */
076        private static void testCreatePostSuccess(PostList postList) {
077                Post post = new Post(1, "Homework 2 CRUD question", "How should we store post subsets?", "CSE360", "student1", false);
078                String error = postList.createPost(post);
079                assertTrue(error == null, "Create post success", "Expected no error, got: " + error);
080        }
081
082        /**
083         * Test ID: T2 (maps to R2).
084         *
085         * <p>Attempts to create a post using an existing ID and verifies duplicate-ID rejection.
086         * PASS means uniqueness validation is enforced.
087         */
088        private static void testCreatePostDuplicateId(PostList postList) {
089                Post duplicate = new Post(1, "Another title", "Duplicate ID should fail", "CSE360", "student2", false);
090                String error = postList.createPost(duplicate);
091                assertTrue("Post ID already exists.".equals(error), "Create duplicate post ID", "Expected duplicate ID message, got: " + error);
092        }
093
094        /**
095         * Test ID: T3 (maps to R3).
096         *
097         * <p>Attempts to create a post with a blank title and verifies validation error.
098         * PASS means title-required validation is enforced.
099         */
100        private static void testCreatePostInvalidTitle(PostList postList) {
101                Post badPost = new Post(2, "   ", "Body exists", "CSE360", "student3", false);
102                String error = postList.createPost(badPost);
103                assertTrue("Post title is required.".equals(error), "Create post invalid title", "Expected title required message, got: " + error);
104        }
105
106        /**
107         * Test ID: T4 (maps to R4).
108         *
109         * <p>Reads an existing post by ID and verifies a non-null result.
110         * PASS means read-by-id behavior returns expected record.
111         */
112        private static void testReadPostById(PostList postList) {
113                Post found = postList.readPostById(1);
114                assertTrue(found != null, "Read post by ID", "Expected post with ID 1");
115        }
116
117        /**
118         * Test ID: T5 (maps to R5).
119         *
120         * <p>Updates an existing post and verifies resolved state and error-free update.
121         * PASS means update writes valid values to target post.
122         */
123        private static void testUpdatePostSuccess(PostList postList) {
124                String error = postList.updatePost(1, "Homework 2 CRUD answer", "Use one list for all and one for subset.", "CSE360", true);
125                Post updated = postList.readPostById(1);
126                boolean ok = error == null && updated != null && updated.isResolved();
127                assertTrue(ok, "Update post success", "Expected successful update");
128        }
129
130        /**
131         * Test ID: T6 (maps to R6).
132         *
133         * <p>Attempts update on a non-existent post ID and verifies missing-target message.
134         * PASS means update correctly rejects unknown IDs.
135         */
136        private static void testUpdatePostMissing(PostList postList) {
137                String error = postList.updatePost(999, "Missing", "Missing", "CSE360", false);
138                assertTrue("Post not found for update.".equals(error), "Update missing post", "Expected missing post message, got: " + error);
139        }
140
141        /**
142         * Test ID: T7 (maps to R7).
143         *
144         * <p>Creates then deletes a post and verifies delete returns true.
145         * PASS means delete removes an existing record.
146         */
147        private static void testDeletePostSuccess(PostList postList) {
148                Post temp = new Post(3, "Temporary", "Delete me", "CSE360", "student4", false);
149                postList.createPost(temp);
150                boolean deleted = postList.deletePost(3);
151                assertTrue(deleted, "Delete post success", "Expected delete true");
152        }
153
154        /**
155         * Test ID: T8 (maps to R8).
156         *
157         * <p>Attempts delete on a non-existent ID and verifies return value is false.
158         * PASS means delete missing-target behavior is correct.
159         */
160        private static void testDeletePostMissing(PostList postList) {
161                boolean deleted = postList.deletePost(999);
162                assertTrue(!deleted, "Delete post missing", "Expected delete false");
163        }
164
165        /**
166         * Test ID: T9 (maps to R9).
167         *
168         * <p>Creates a reply referencing an existing post and verifies no error.
169         * PASS means reply-create with valid post linkage is supported.
170         */
171        private static void testCreateReplySuccess(PostList postList, ReplyList replyList) {
172                Reply reply = new Reply(1, 1, "Store subset in a second list and refresh after search.", "staff01");
173                String error = replyList.createReplyForPost(reply, postList);
174                assertTrue(error == null, "Create reply success", "Expected no error, got: " + error);
175        }
176
177        /**
178         * Test ID: T10 (maps to R10).
179         *
180         * <p>Attempts reply creation with an invalid post ID and verifies rejection.
181         * PASS means referential integrity is enforced for replies.
182         */
183        private static void testCreateReplyInvalidPostReference(PostList postList, ReplyList replyList) {
184                Reply badReply = new Reply(2, 999, "This should fail because post does not exist.", "staff02");
185                String error = replyList.createReplyForPost(badReply, postList);
186                assertTrue("Reply must reference an existing post.".equals(error), "Create reply invalid post", "Expected missing-post message, got: " + error);
187        }
188
189        /**
190         * Test ID: T11 (maps to R11).
191         *
192         * <p>Attempts to update a reply with a blank body and verifies validation error.
193         * PASS means reply-body validation is enforced on update.
194         */
195        private static void testUpdateReplyInvalidBody(ReplyList replyList) {
196                String error = replyList.updateReply(1, "   ");
197                assertTrue("Reply body is required.".equals(error), "Update reply invalid body", "Expected reply body required message, got: " + error);
198        }
199
200        /**
201         * Test ID: T12 (maps to R12).
202         *
203         * <p>Builds subset views for posts/replies and verifies expected subset sizes.
204         * PASS means search/filter subset mechanics are functioning.
205         */
206        private static void testSubsetSearch(PostList postList, ReplyList replyList) {
207                postList.createPost(new Post(4, "Project setup", "How do I run maven test?", "CSE360", "student5", false));
208                postList.setSubsetFromKeyword("maven");
209
210                replyList.createReplyForPost(new Reply(3, 4, "Use mvn test in the project root.", "staff03"), postList);
211                replyList.setSubsetFromPostId(4);
212
213                boolean ok = postList.readSubsetPosts().size() == 1 && replyList.readSubsetReplies().size() == 1;
214                assertTrue(ok, "Subset search", "Expected one post and one reply in subset");
215        }
216
217        /**
218         * Assertion helper used by all built-in tests.
219         *
220         * @param condition test condition to verify
221         * @param testName display name of test case
222         * @param failureDetails message shown on failure
223         */
224        private static void assertTrue(boolean condition, String testName, String failureDetails) {
225                if (condition) {
226                        passed++;
227                        System.out.println("PASS: " + testName);
228                } else {
229                        failed++;
230                        System.out.println("FAIL: " + testName + " -> " + failureDetails);
231                }
232        }
233}
